title: nauclea orientalis
intro: Nauclea orientalis, also known as Quinine Tree or Kakuam, is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antimalarial properties, making it effective in treating fever, malaria, and other diseases. The health benefits of Nauclea orientalis include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and improving overall immune system function. The herbal preparations of Nauclea orientalis include dried leaves, extracts, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", nauclea orientalis (also referred to as Nauclea latifolia Smith) is effective in treating malaria fever and painful conditions due to its anti-nociceptive, anti-inflammatory and anti-pyretic activities.
